topic: Areas of Inquiry: Music for Sociocultural and Political Expression
subject: music

items.correct: Rapid alternation between the written note and the note a step above.
items.why: Students confuse trill (rapid alternation between two notes) with vibrato (pitch oscillation around one note). Trills add ornamentation; vibrato adds expressive width to a sustained pitch.

items.q: What is characteristic of Baroque music?
items.correct: Continuous bass line, ornamentation, terraced dynamics, and counterpoint.
items.why: Students project later styles backwards. Baroque (1600-1750) used basso continuo, harpsichord/organ, and terraced (sudden) dynamics. Piano hadn't yet replaced harpsichord.

items.q: How many semitones are in a perfect fifth?
items.why: Students count fifth as 'five steps' but the name refers to letter NAMES (1-2-3-4-5), not semitones. C to G spans 5 letter names but 7 semitones (C-C#-D-D#-E-F-F#-G).

items.q: In what year was the world music label adopted by indie labels?
items.why: Correct answer: 1987. “1972” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How many areas of inquiry frame IBDP Music since 2020?
items.why: The real figure is 4 areas. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
